summary: Replicaset timeout event
Finding documents from a collection with non-ASCII collection name returns an empty array
Options parameter in MongoClient.connect(url, options) does not set values.
FindOne doesn't return cursor object
description:
The method have to return a cursor object according to documentation, but it doesn't return.

https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/2.0/lib/collection.js#L825

Thanks!

summary: Kerberos library for Node Driver does not target Linux environments
description:
Hi,

The bindings.gyp used by the Kerberos library for the Node driver does not target Linux platforms (it only targets OSX and Windows). Can this be packaged / rebuilt to include Linux platforms ?

The Kerberos library is:
 * https://github.com/christkv/kerberos/blob/master/binding.gyp

Thanks!
Eoin

summary: Db is not prototype-based
description:
Continuing #309, it seems most things are now prototype-based, but Db is not. Is there a reason that was not converted too? Presently, I can't get at any of the collection, user and index management methods on it without significant monkey-patching.

Also, can you expose Cursor in index.js too? It's much easier to patch when the constructor is accessible somewhere.

summary: Many trivial errors with 2.0 document
description:
http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.0/api-docs/

For example, see Collection method `find` and `findOne`, seems like copy-paste errors.

http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.0/tutorials/crud_operations/

There are instances of mix up whether removeOne/Many or deleteOne/Many should be used, api says deleteX, tutorial says removeX. While old remove() is  being deprecated, but delete() does not exists.

summary: 'no valid seed servers in list'  when using MongoClient.connect() for replica set on Ubuntu
Uncaught ReferenceError in /lib/db open()
description:
Line 189 of /lib/db.js has an uncaught error in the open() method.

Should be `return;`, but was accidentally typed as returnl`.

Easy to repro by just not passing a callback to the open method.

summary: Bulk insert error not an instance of Error
description:
When an error occurs during an insert to MongoDB 2.6, a {{WriteError}} is returned in the callback instead of an {{Error}} (so {{err.message}} or {{err.stack}} is undefined).

{{[insertWithWriteCommands()|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/9cff3953c3cf6a81439b8ae4f4dbd7a583ded142/lib/mongodb/collection/core.js#L42]}} does not call {{util.toError()}} [here|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/9cff3953c3cf6a81439b8ae4f4dbd7a583ded142/lib/mongodb/collection/core.js#L118], [here|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/9cff3953c3cf6a81439b8ae4f4dbd7a583ded142/lib/mongodb/collection/core.js#L120], [here|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/9cff3953c3cf6a81439b8ae4f4dbd7a583ded142/lib/mongodb/collection/core.js#L147] and [here|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/9cff3953c3cf6a81439b8ae4f4dbd7a583ded142/lib/mongodb/collection/core.js#L149].

summary: issue with mongo.Long.toString()
description:
mongo.Long initialized with a big number behave strange. E.G.
[tong@localhost source]$ node
> var mongo = require('mongodb')
undefined
> mongo.Long.fromNumber(52126039501605650).toString()
'52126039501605648'       //strange thing. look like a bug here.
> mongo.Long.fromNumber(52126039501605650).toNumber()
52126039501605650

summary: Cannot create index for nested objects
description:
When i create an index with the command

collection.ensureIndex("c.d", callback);

i get the following error:

{ [MongoError: n/a]
  name: 'MongoError',
  message: 'n/a',
  ok: 1,
  n: 1,
  code: 14,
  errmsg: 'key c.d must not contain \'.\'',
  writeErrors: [ { index: 0, code: 14, errmsg: 'key c.d must not contain \'.\'' } ] }

summary: Use setImmediate instead of process.nextTick
description:
Pro:
process.nextTick insert on the head the callback destroying the nature of the nodejs stack.
It's better to use setImmadiate to add on the tick stack all callbacks.
Cons:
setImmediate is slower than process.nextTick

summary: Can't call require('mongodb').MongoClient in 2 different JS files in the same NodeJS instance
description:
We created a Mongo_dao.js for our webapp to handle data retrieval using our MongoDB. Created a Mongo_dao_test.js to integration test our Mongo_dao.js.  Both files contain require('mongodb').MongoClient in the code. 

The integration test tries to setup some collections in a Mongo DB instance, and then instantiates a Mongo_dao instance. What appears is happening is that the first line of code that gets to require('mongodb').MongoClient first gets a working copy of the library. Any other code after that uses require('mongodb').MongoClient acts as if the MongoDB it is talking to is empty and never stores any data. In other words, it act like a NULL instance.

If there is only one JS file in the entire NodeJS instance that uses require('mongodb').MongoClient it seems to work just fine.

summary: documentation update and query functions is out of date
description:
the documentation for $collection->query looks like (note $limit, $skip):

=method query ($query)

when in fact it should be

=method query ($query, $limit, $skip)

Likewise, the $collection->update method, specifies:

=method update ($object, $upsert?)

Should be (note $query):

 =method update ($query, $object, $upsert)

summary: querying database lacks ability to sort
description: it's not currently possible (or obvious how to) to get a sorted result back as part of a query.

summary: perl module #define's VERSION which conflicts with stdafx.h in the mongo sdk
description: when trying to compile the perl module from source, it does a -DVERSION=... but this define exists in stdafx.h already. the work-around is temporarily renaming the stdafx define to something like VERSION_

summary: Doesn't allow double's to saved
description:
Inserting { foo => 0.123 } gives a "type unhandled" error.

The commit at http://github.com/abh/mongo-perl-driver/commit/3f754d68e0eda12f365d7b7c98a7c78e43533cf0 fixes it.

summary: add stringification to OID
Check for mongo libs in normal include paths
description: Right now it can only be set via MONGO_SDK, which doesn't work well when the includes/lib are in /usr/local

summary: specify include and lib path separately instead of (or in addition to) just MONGO_SDK
description: this way you can build against either an SDK or a src distribution

summary: No error reporting on duplicate errors
Add fallback => 1; to the overload line
description: Sorry about the lack of an actual diff file, but at line 54 or so of lib/MongoDB/OID.pm add the key "fallback => 1" to the "use overload" statement. This allows Perl to guess which overloaded operators to use in certain conditions.

summary: Git repo current reversion (99271aa) catastrophically fails test suite on Debian lenny
description: Checking out the latest git version and running make test both fails the test and appears to suffer from internal data corruption issues. Test results attached..

summary: No GridFS
Fix pod
Uninitialised array values not supported
description:
Trying to insert arrays with uninitialised values blows up.

my @g = ();
$g[1] = 'foo';
$coll->insert({ data => \@g })

gives: failed to fetch array value at /code/MongoDB/Connection.pm line 205.

A patch to vivify uninitialised elements as undef is here:
http://github.com/yesdave/mongo-perl-driver/commit/21a36ebbc25d7c4777be84885ea2b156429a0c17

summary: PVNV type not supported
description:
SV's of type PVNV aren't currently supported. 
Attempting to save or query using a scalar variable that was a float and now a string, and vice versa blows up with:

Here is a patch and test case to support this type:
http://github.com/yesdave/mongo-perl-driver/commit/0ade3be96c4a2dc8ba36552f426429d50223d07d

summary: Module::Build's 'inc' dir is not included
description:
Module::Build's 'inc' directory is not included in the CPAN tarball, leading to failure on install. Installing Module::Install solved the problem, but the whole point of M::I and its inc/ directory is that the user is not *required* to have M::I installed; only the developer :)

summary: rt.cpan.org#52648 - MongoDB 0.26 and Perl 5.8.7, Newx(z) with 3 args
description:
Hello and thank you for MongoDB 0.26,

I also created a rt.cpan.org ticket that contains the same information, hope you don't mind me also creating this issue, I wasn't sure which you would prefer.

  https://rt.cpan.org/Ticket/Display.html?id=52648

I'm running Perl 5.8.7 (on x86_64-linux)

Running make produces:

cc -c -I. -fno-strict-aliasing -pipe -I/usr/local/include
-D_LARGEFILE_SOURCE -D_FILE_OFFSET_BITS=64 -I/usr/include/gdbm -O2
-DVERSION=\"0.26\" -DXS_VERSION=\"0.26\" -o xs/Connection.o -fpic
"-I<removed>perl-5.8.7/lib/5.8.7/x86_64-linux/CORE" xs/Connection.c
xs/Connection.xs: In function `XS_MongoDB__Connection_connect':
xs/Connection.xs:52: error: syntax error before "mongo_link"
xs/Connection.xs:63: error: syntax error before "char"
xs/Connection.xs:68: error: syntax error before "char"
xs/Connection.xs:75: error: syntax error before "char"
xs/Connection.xs: In function `XS_MongoDB__Connection__insert':
xs/Connection.xs:118: error: syntax error before "char"
xs/Connection.xs: In function `XS_MongoDB__Connection__remove':
xs/Connection.xs:147: error: syntax error before "char"
xs/Connection.xs: In function `XS_MongoDB__Connection__update':
xs/Connection.xs:170: error: syntax error before "char"
make: *** [xs/Connection.o] Error 1

Line 52 of xs/Connection.xs is:

Newx(link, 1, mongo_link);

I'm using version 2.21 of ExtUtils::ParseXS

Line 122 of xs/Connection.c is:

Newx(link, 1, mongo_link);

According to http://perldoc.perl.org/perlapi.html In 5.9.3, Newx() and
friends replace the older New() API, and drops the first parameter, x, a
debug aid which allowed callers to identify themselves. Looking at
perlapi for 5.8.7
(http://search.cpan.org/~nwclark/perl-5.8.7/pod/perlapi.pod) I see:

New
The XSUB-writer's interface to the C malloc function.
void New(int id, void* ptr, int nitems, type)

No mention of Newx, so I changed line 52 of xs/Connection.xs to:

New(0, link, 1, mongo_link);

and this seemed to work, I also made the following changes:

MongoDB-0.26/mongo_link.c
245c245
> New(0, cursor->buf.start, cursor->header.length, char);
---
< Newx(cursor->buf.start, cursor->header.length, char);

MongoDB-0.26/mongo_link.h
88c88
> New(0, buf.start, size, char); \
---
< Newx(buf.start, size, char); \

MongoDB-0.26/xs/Connection.xs
52c52
> New(0, link, 1, mongo_link);
---
< Newx(link, 1, mongo_link);
63c63
> Newz(0, link->server.pair.left_host, llen+1, char);
---
< Newxz(link->server.pair.left_host, llen+1, char);
68c68
> Newz(0, link->server.pair.right_host, rlen+1, char);
---
< Newxz(link->server.pair.right_host, rlen+1, char);
75c75
> Newz(0, link->server.single.host, len+1, char);
---
< Newxz(link->server.single.host, len+1, char);

MongoDB-0.26/xs/Cursor.xs
115,116c115
> New(0, buf.start, size, char);
---
< Newx(buf.start, size, char);
190c189
> New(0, cursor, 1, mongo_cursor);
---
< Newx(cursor, 1, mongo_cursor);

make now completes without error and make test when running against the
stable version of mongod just fails 4 tests in t/collection.t (I have
not looked into these failures yet)

not ok 74
# Failed test at t/collection.t line 256.
# got: '1'
# expected: '2'
ok 75
not ok 76
# Failed test at t/collection.t line 263.
# got: undef
# expected: '4'
not ok 77
# Failed test at t/collection.t line 267.
# got: '1'
# expected: '2'
ok 78
not ok 79
# Failed test at t/collection.t line 274.
# got: undef
# expected: '4'

I've not tested the change with any other version of perl.

Hope this is of some use.

Cheers,
Peter (Stig) Edwards

Strawberry Perl has no int64_t so MongoDB won't install
description:
ashb in the #mongodb irc channel recommended I use Strawberry Perl on windows instead of ActiveState

But MongoDB.pm won't install: Strawberry Perl doesn't seem to provide int64_t:

./mongo_link.h:123: error: syntax error before "int64_t"

summary: Modification of non-creatable hash value attempted
description:
An error can occur when dumping or serializing hashes returned from methods in this module when a hash value is undef.

For example:

  Data::Dump::dump $database->last_error();

will generate the following error:

Modification of non-creatable hash value attempted, subscript "err" at /usr/local/share/perl/5.10.0/Data/Dump.pm line 272.

if the return value is:

{
  'ok' => '1',
  'n' => 1,
  'err' => undef,
  'updatedExisting' => 1
}

This is a fairly common bug encountered when using dumpers or serializers (like YAML etc)...

Now I'm no XS expert but I've heard that newSV(0) can be used in place of &PL_sv_undef to alleviate the problem (see http://search.cpan.org/~dapm/perl-5.10.1/pod/perlguts.pod#AVs,_HVs_and_undefined_values).

There are two places in the code that need to be changed, and it seems to work... but hopefully someone with expert XS knowledge can verify that it really is that easy to fix (I'm reluctant to make a patch without such an opinion).

perl_mongo.c line 334
xs/Cursor.xs line 236

summary: Development releases of mongo-perl-driver should use developer version strings (like 0.27_01)
description:
also, I noticed that the latest update to mongo-perl-driver didn't bump the version string
<kristina1> on github?
<centosian2> yup
<kristina1> i don't bump the version string until i release the package on cpan

----
<centosian2> hm, I think the 'normal' thing to do (to avoid having different versions floating around with the same vstring) is to number it like 0.27_01
----

<centosian2> lemme find a reference
<kristina1> i'm open to it, although it'll be a little annoying to change the six zillion places there's a version every time i make a commit
<centosian2> wow, you'er not kidding. Maybe have them all fetch it from MongoDB::Version ? Or use the one in MongoDB.pm
<centosian2> "Versions which are still being implemented usually have the current version number with an underscore and a number, like 0.04_01 and are called "developer releases"; the underscore makes it clear that the distribution is not yet ready for the general public. You should follow that practice. You can even upload a developer's release to the CPAN. When people look at your module they will see that version, with a red sign stating it's a developer's rel
<centosian2> ease and a link to the last ready-to-take version. 
<centosian2> from http://www.perlmonks.org/?node_id=431702
<kristina1> cool, i'll start doing that
<centosian2> awesome. Want me to file a JIRA about it?
<kristina1> yeah, that would be great
<centosian2> ok one sec
